expectations.text: Recommends against directly injecting the public IFormSubmissionAppService across module boundaries
Recommends ABP Integration Services as the right pattern, explicitly mentioning the [IntegrationService] attribute
expectations.evidence: The correct pattern in an ABP **layered modular monolith** is an **Integration Service** ... `[IntegrationService] public interface IFormSubmissionIntegrationService : IApplicationService`

expectations.text: Defines the integration-service interface in the producing module's *.Application.Contracts (or *.Contracts) project
expectations.evidence: ### 1. Interface + DTO — in `Acme.Forms.Application.Contracts` ... This is the only Forms project Reports is allowed to reference.

expectations.text: Shows the implementation in the producing module's *.Application project (or equivalent), inheriting ApplicationService and implementing the [IntegrationService] interface
expectations.evidence: ### 2. Implementation — in `Acme.Forms.Application` ... `public class FormSubmissionIntegrationService : ApplicationService, IFormSubmissionIntegrationService`

expectations.text: Notes that integration services are hidden from the public API surface (and/or audit-off by default / not [Authorize] by default)
expectations.evidence: By default, integration services are **not** exposed as auto-API endpoints (you opt in with `ExposeIntegrationServices = true` only if you actually want them on HTTP — in a single-process monolith you usually don't). ... Authorization on integration services is typically not the UI permission ... or omit `[Authorize]`

expectations.text: Explains the in-process modular-monolith vs microservice transport difference (DI in-process now vs HTTP / generated proxy across services), framed as 'same contract, different transport'
expectations.evidence: ## Modular-monolith vs microservice: the transport difference ... **the contract stays identical; only the transport changes.** ... DI resolves `IFormSubmissionIntegrationService` to the **concrete `FormSubmissionIntegrationService`** ... [microservice] DI now resolves the interface to the generated **HTTP client proxy**

expectations.text: Specifies that the consumer module's AbpModule depends only on the producer's *.Application.Contracts module (e.g. [DependsOn(typeof(AcmeFormsApplicationContractsModule))] on the Reports.Application module) — NOT on the producer's full *.Application module. This contracts-only dependency is the load-bearing detail that preserves the module boundary
expectations.evidence: Reports adds a `[DependsOn]` on the Forms **Application.Contracts module**, and only on that. It must never depend on `Acme.Forms.Application` or `Acme.Forms.EntityFrameworkCore`. ... `// Cross-module dependency: ONLY the Contracts module of Forms.` `typeof(AcmeFormsApplicationContractsModule)`
